summary: Proof-worth ratchet registry for touched executable tests, helpers, fixtures, and proof assets. V0 seeds only the enforced dev-workflow and recent wait/proof families.

entries.contract: The proof-worth registry schema, canonical registry, fixtures, and evaluator smoke coverage stay aligned.
entries.worth: Without this proof, the ratchet can silently stop enforcing the metadata it depends on.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: The dev workflow rules manifest and fixtures validate and preserve V0 routing contracts.
entries.worth: This catches stale routing rules before agents trust a bad recommendation packet.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: The active development profile selector points at a defined workflow profile.
entries.worth: This keeps developer routing profile selection explicit instead of implied by stale defaults.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Workflow profile manifests and fixtures remain schema-valid.
entries.worth: This prevents profile metadata drift from changing the dev routing context silently.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: The dev classify/recommend router, help form parity, and proof-worth ratchet behavior agree.
entries.worth: This is the cheapest end-to-end proof that changed paths route to actionable validation instead of stale broad loops.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: The dev grammar verifier reports evidence-backed pass and fail claims for manifest, help, and recommendation routing.
entries.worth: This keeps the hidden maintainer router self-auditing instead of trusting stale help or manifest assumptions.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: The dev situation packet preserves sourced repo, GitHub, readiness, successor-note, and partial-failure state without synthesizing missing facts.
entries.worth: This protects the operator orientation surface agents use before choosing a workflow route.
entries.guard: uses fake AOS and temporary Git repositories

entries.contract: The durable-status tripwire distinguishes dated historical claims from unmarked standing lifecycle claims.
entries.worth: This prevents stale issue, stash, runtime, and role claims from becoming active instructions through docs drift.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Source command manifests and generated compatibility artifacts stay in sync.
entries.worth: This is the cheapest proof that command metadata edits did not bypass generation.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: External command manifest schema, script targets, and help-manifest route coverage remain valid.
entries.worth: This catches command schema drift without rebuilding or starting runtime services.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Manifest-backed command routes dispatch through their external scripts and wrappers.
entries.worth: This catches route drift where generated command manifests still validate but runtime dispatch reaches the wrong implementation.
entries.guard: uses bounded CLI dispatch and isolated state for daemon-sensitive checks

entries.contract: External command wrappers reject malformed flags and arguments before falling through to native primitives.
entries.worth: This keeps parser failures explicit and prevents unknown input from reaching lower-level command adapters.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and disables daemon autostart

entries.contract: Discoverable help, public command docs, parser errors, and generated command registry metadata stay aligned.
entries.worth: This is the broad command-surface guard that catches stale help or docs after manifest and adapter changes.
entries.guard: read-only help and parser checks

entries.contract: Script-owned dev gh subcommands stay dispatchable and discoverable through real ./aos help.
entries.worth: This keeps the sanctioned GitHub workflow subset discoverable without exposing unsupported gh prompts or hidden paths.
entries.guard: read-only help and source inspection

entries.contract: Repo-mode builds are content-based, do not post-sign, and emit the manual TCC handoff only after real rebuilds.
entries.worth: This protects rebuild behavior without touching the real repo-mode binary or macOS TCC state.
entries.guard: uses a temporary fake repo and fake toolchain

entries.contract: Generic visual primitives and app-specific visual compositions stay separated.
entries.worth: This prevents shared visual helpers from absorbing product-specific launch behavior.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Canonical aos:// URL generation and resolved URL equivalence remain deterministic.
entries.worth: This keeps visual harness checks representative of the current content-root path.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Stale live content-root detection remains deterministic before visual proof runs.
entries.worth: This catches stale runtime content before visual checks produce misleading evidence.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Shared harness guards fail fast and release cleanly.
entries.worth: This prevents visual helper composition from leaving hidden daemon or canvas state behind.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Root skill registry coverage, retired-skill tombstones, and split installable skill contracts remain valid.
entries.worth: This prevents installable skill metadata from drifting away from the registry validator and package bodies.
entries.guard: read-only skill package inspection

entries.contract: Skills efficacy scoring, prompt packet generation, captured OpenAI response handling, and CLI threshold behavior remain deterministic.
entries.worth: This catches drift between installable skill guidance, generated command manifests, fixture captures, and scorer boundaries before live model runs are trusted.
entries.guard: offline fixture; live OpenAI capture is mocked in the test

entries.contract: The captured agentic-efficacy fixture evaluates through the public CLI JSON path without live provider access.
entries.worth: This proves the fixture remains usable by maintainers outside the unit harness and keeps live-provider capture separate from deterministic scoring.
entries.guard: offline captured fixture; does not use --run-openai

entries.contract: Developer capability manifest schema, canonical capability set, raw-process constraints, and GitHub capability metadata stay aligned.
entries.worth: This keeps typed developer capabilities explicit instead of falling back to raw process assumptions.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Content-root wait success, parser rejection, autostart, and missing-root timeout shape stay bounded.
entries.worth: This protects the canonical content wait surface without touching the shared repo daemon.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT

entries.contract: Linked git worktrees cannot use the default repo runtime without an explicit isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT.
entries.worth: This protects the single-checkout runtime ownership model from alternate-checkout agent work.
entries.guard: creates a disposable linked worktree and does not execute its native ./aos binary

entries.contract: Content wait polling does not accumulate socket listeners while status is unresolved.
entries.worth: This catches listener leaks with a fake socket instead of a live daemon loop.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Show wait bounds daemon read stalls by caller timeout and emits structured pending-condition JSON.
entries.worth: This proves timeout behavior without needing a real canvas or live repo daemon.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Show wait observes a URL-backed toolkit canvas readiness predicate through an isolated daemon.
entries.worth: This is the focused live smoke for the user-visible wait path without sharing the repo runtime.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT

entries.contract: Live-start operations fail closed without explicit allow-start authority.
entries.worth: This keeps content wait, show wait, launch, and experience activation from mutating runtime state accidentally.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and asserts live start is blocked

entries.contract: Ready, status, doctor, and clean classify stale daemons consistently.
entries.worth: This keeps readiness proof bounded to mock and temporary daemon state instead of broad live cleanup.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT plus temporary mock processes

entries.contract: The wait/surface housecleaning report remains an evidence pointer, not a deletion authority by itself.
entries.worth: This protects test cleanup decisions from deleting a proof before its replacement command is green and routed.
entries.guard: report-only; run the named replacement proof before deleting a test

entries.contract: Real-input surface scenarios use one guarded helper for readiness, canvas existence, cleanup, and input preflight.
entries.worth: This keeps shared real-input sleeps and readiness polling isolated behind a named helper instead of spreading them through broad default tests.
entries.guard: requires explicit real-input approval, idle mouse and keyboard, active repo daemon readiness, and harness lock availability

entries.contract: Sigil radial menu responds to real pointer input through the active repo daemon and Surface Inspector harness.
entries.worth: This catches pointer-delivery and radial-hit-target failures that renderer or show-eval tests cannot prove.
entries.guard: requires AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, idle mouse and keyboard, active repo daemon readiness, and harness lock availability

entries.contract: Sigil radial menu handles a real-input DesktopWorld path through the active repo daemon and Surface Inspector harness.
entries.worth: This protects DesktopWorld coordinate conversion and radial-path delivery beyond deterministic renderer state.
entries.guard: requires AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, idle mouse and keyboard, active repo daemon readiness, and harness lock availability

entries.contract: Sigil status-item click launches the avatar through real input in an isolated daemon state root.
entries.worth: This proves status-item ownership and click delivery without relying on renderer state mutation alone.
entries.guard: requires AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, idle mouse and keyboard, status-item access, and isolated daemon setup

entries.contract: Sigil avatar controls can be operated through AOS-native real-input control records.
entries.worth: This catches native control-record click failures that compact panel state tests cannot observe.
entries.guard: requires AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, idle mouse and keyboard, native input permissions, and isolated daemon setup

entries.contract: Surface Inspector launcher creates a URL-backed canvas and waits for live lifecycle plus display geometry readiness.
entries.worth: This catches WebView import, content-root, canvas placement, and Surface Inspector readiness failures that Node-only tests miss.
entries.guard: requires live AOS readiness or explicit --allow-start; not part of broad default loops

entries.contract: Wiki Subject Browser launcher creates a graph-first workbench canvas with subject search, filters, and details refs.
entries.worth: This catches content-root, fixture, and workbench boot failures not covered by package-local logic tests.
entries.guard: requires live AOS readiness and repo/toolkit content roots; not part of broad default loops

entries.contract: Step Descriptor Workbench launcher opens fixture-backed descriptor and evidence state in a URL-backed canvas.
entries.worth: This catches fixture handoff and workbench boot failures beyond schema validation alone.
entries.guard: requires live AOS readiness and toolkit content root; not part of broad default loops

entries.contract: Artifact Bundle Workbench launcher opens a fixture-backed artifact subject in a URL-backed canvas.
entries.worth: This catches artifact fixture projection, repo content-root, and workbench boot failures outside Node-only checks.
entries.guard: requires live AOS readiness and repo/toolkit content roots; not part of broad default loops

entries.contract: Work Record Workbench launcher opens fixture-backed Work Record state in a URL-backed canvas.
entries.worth: This catches Work Record workbench boot and fixture loading failures not covered by schema tests alone.
entries.guard: requires live AOS readiness or explicit --allow-start; not part of broad default loops

entries.contract: Cross-backend saved-ref regression proof preserves browser, AOS canvas, native AX, summary, and cleanup evidence.
entries.worth: This proof is valuable but too environment-sensitive for broad default loops.
entries.guard: requires explicit guarded-live approval, isolated proof root, and permission/runtime readiness

entries.contract: Native AX saved-ref live proof exercises the real native accessibility boundary.
entries.worth: This catches native-boundary failures that deterministic saved-ref fixtures cannot observe.
entries.guard: requires explicit real-input approval, native AX permissions, and a user-safe desktop state
